english: Yuan Yang instant coffee tea mix
Instant yuan yang with creamer and sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for yuan yang instant premix; mapped to nearest instant beverage class. FoodOn does not have a dedicated term for coffee-tea blend premix as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coffee and coffee substitutes — instant coffee products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: Yuan yang is a coffee-tea blend; FoodEx2 A0FKP covers instant coffee products and is the closest defensible anchor. No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for coffee-tea premix blends.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for instant coffee-tea premix beverage as of 2024.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet of 3-in-1 yuan yang instant beverage weighs approximately 25 g and is dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water to yield one cup.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Yuan Yang — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for 3-in-1 yuan yang (cr_id D09010015). Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 (Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar) as the closest approved analogue — saturated fat approximately 1.8 g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and water not reported by HPB or USDA analogue at this level of detail and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for 3-in-1 yuan yang instant beverage in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous product: sweetened instant coffee-tea premix with non-dairy creamer and sugar, consistent with GI values reported for sugar-sweetened instant beverage mixes (GI 60–70) in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 65 × 12.94 × 0.25 / 100 × 100 = 65 × 3.235 / 100 = 2.1 per 25 g sachet serving. GL = 65 × 3.235 / 100 = 2.1. Note: GL reported as 8.4 per 100 g equivalent for comparability (65 × 12.94 / 100 = 8.41).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (10.22 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain; sodium is low at 26 mg/100 g but provides no meaningful offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid 3-in-1 yuan yang due to its high sugar load (10.22 g/100 g), which exceeds the AHA 2018 threshold for SSBs and independently elevates blood pressure risk; switch to plain unsweetened brewed coffee-tea without added sugar or creamer.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 10.22 g/100 g with dietary fibre only 0.55 g/100 g in a liquid matrix produces rapid glycaemic excursion; medium GI of 65 combined with liquid delivery accelerates glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product as its high sugar content (10.22 g/100 g), low fibre (0.55 g/100 g), and medium GI (65) in liquid form cause rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes; ADA guidelines classify all sweetened instant beverage mixes as unsuitable for diabetes management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-dairy creamer contributes saturated fat (estimated 1.8 g/100 g) and may contain partially hydrogenated vegetable oil; high sugar (10.22 g/100 g) promotes dyslipidaemia and visceral adiposity.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid 3-in-1 yuan yang due to its saturated fat from non-dairy creamer and high added sugar content, both of which adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk; choose plain unsweetened coffee or tea without creamer.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar (10.22 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; non-dairy creamer likely contributes palm-based saturated fat and possible trans fat, outweighing any polyphenol benefit from coffee and tea.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product as its high sugar and saturated fat content promote systemic inflammation; a plain unsweetened coffee-tea blend without creamer would provide polyphenol benefit without the inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Yuan Yang Instant Beverage (三合一鸳鸯) is a popular Singaporean and Malaysian Chinese instant premix drink that combines instant coffee powder, instant tea powder, non-dairy creamer, and sugar in a single sachet. The name 'yuan yang' (鸳鸯) refers to the traditional Hong Kong-style blend of coffee and milk tea, symbolising the complementary pairing of the two beverages. The 3-in-1 format dissolves instantly in hot water, producing a sweet, creamy, mildly caffeinated beverage with a characteristic bittersweet coffee-tea flavour and a smooth, milky texture from the non-dairy creamer. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as an afternoon pick-me-up across Singapore, Malaysia, and the broader Chinese diaspora in Southeast Asia. Nutritionally, per 100 g of powder, it provides 77 kcal, 0.61 g protein, 2.55 g fat (including approximately 1.8 g saturated fat from non-dairy creamer), and 12.94 g carbohydrate of which 10.22 g is sugar. Dietary fibre is low at 0.55 g/100 g and sodium is low at 26 mg/100 g. The high sugar content (10.22 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The non-dairy creamer fraction typically contains palm oil or partially hydrogenated vegetable oil, contributing saturated and potentially trans fats. Despite the presence of coffee and tea polyphenols, the pro-inflammatory components from sugar and saturated fat dominate the nutritional profile. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 2.1 per standard 25 g sachet serving. This product is ultra-processed and is best consumed only occasionally by healthy individuals.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in 3-in-1 yuan yang?
Can diabetics drink yuan yang instant?
What is yuan yang coffee tea?
Is instant yuan yang suitable for hypertension?
What is in 3-in-1 yuan yang premix?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id D09010015, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest approved analogue (instant coffee with whitener and sugar, NDB 14209). GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous product category. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Calcium is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g and iron as 0.0 mg/100 g per HPB Foodo source; these are confirmed zero values from the approved source for this product formulation. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this instant coffee-tea premix are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central at the required level of detail as of 2026. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g) is an approved-source estimate from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 (Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar), the closest defensible analogue. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened instant beverage mixes reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for yuan yang 3-in-1 was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Halal status assumed based on typical commercial formulation; verify against product label. Vegan flag set to false as non-dairy creamer may contain casein (milk protein derivative). Dietary tags from input (low_sodium, low_calorie, high_sugar_warning) are noted: low_sodium is supported (26 mg/100 g); low_calorie is borderline (77 kcal/100 g powder but reconstituted beverage is lower calorie); high_sugar_warning is confirmed (10.22 g/100 g).
